author: Pantera Claw
targetAudiences: small and mid sized business owners
marketing and sales leaders who are not technical
audienceVoice: Write for a busy business owner who is curious about AI and data but does not write code. Assume they care about revenue, customer experience, time saved, and competitive advantage. Avoid jargon; when a technical term is necessary, explain it in one short sentence.

seoKeywords.instructions: Naturally incorporate 3 to 5 of these keywords into every blog post. Do not force them; only use them where they fit the topic organically.

topicPool: A six step AI plan for small businesses that have never used AI before
Which parts of your business should you automate with AI first, and which to leave alone
How AI customer service is changing in 2026, and what your business should do about it
Using AI to write your marketing copy without sounding like a robot
How to run a competitive analysis in an afternoon using AI
What AI Overviews in Google means for small businesses, and how to show up in them
Citation ready content: how to write so AI tools quote your business
Local SEO in the AI era: getting found when customers ask ChatGPT for recommendations
Search everywhere optimization: why keyword ranking is no longer enough
Multimodal AI explained for business owners: why it matters that AI can see, hear, and read
AI agents vs chatbots: the practical difference for your business
Building an AI powered customer experience that does not feel automated
How to measure whether an AI tool is actually saving your team time
The five AI mistakes small business owners make in year one, and how to avoid them
How AI is changing pricing decisions for small and mid sized businesses
What a realistic AI budget looks like for a ten person company
How to brief an AI tool so it writes in your brand voice
The quiet revolution in email and newsletters thanks to AI
When an AI agent is the right tool, and when a spreadsheet still wins
Three questions to ask any vendor selling you an AI solution

style.tone: professional but approachable, plain spoken, confident without hype

formatting.noDashesNote: Never use hyphens or dashes (-) in post content. Use commas, semicolons, or rewrite the sentence instead.
formatting.headings: Use 3 to 5 h2 headings to break the post into scannable sections. Do not use h1 in the body; the page renders the title separately.
formatting.lists: Use short bulleted lists where they genuinely help a business reader scan. Do not list for the sake of it.

heroImage.instructions: Every post must have a heroImage. Use a Picsum seed URL in the format https://picsum.photos/seed/<slug>/1200/675 so posts always render; a human can replace with a curated Unsplash image later.
